message:
🐛 Fix leaderboard pagination rendering duplicate rows + taller Pareto card (#941)

The Hardware Leaderboard table rendered more rows than the page size (e.g. 30
rows instead of 10) because of duplicate React keys. Root cause:

1. `generateLeaderboardRows` used `reports[i]` to reference the source report,
   but `i` is the index into the filtered `points` array (from
   `extractParetoPoints`), not the original `reports` array. This caused
   multiple leaderboard rows to reference the same report object.

2. The table used `row.report.run.uid` as the React key. Since multiple rows
   got the same report reference, they shared the same key, causing React's
   reconciler to render phantom DOM nodes.

Fixes:
- Build a uid→report Map for correct report lookups in generateLeaderboardRows
- Use `row.rank` as the unique React key (rank is assigned sequentially)
- Increase Pareto Frontier default height from h:5 to h:10

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-    { id: 'bench-pareto-1', cardType: 'pareto_frontier', title: 'Pareto Frontier', position: { w: 12, h: 5 } },
+    { id: 'bench-pareto-1', cardType: 'pareto_frontier', title: 'Pareto Frontier', position: { w: 12, h: 10 } },
     { id: 'bench-leaderboard-1', cardType: 'hardware_leaderboard', title: 'Hardware Leaderboard', position: { w: 12, h: 5 } },
     { id: 'bench-latency-1', cardType: 'latency_breakdown', title: 'Latency Breakdown', position: { w: 12, h: 4 } },
     { id: 'bench-throughput-1', cardType: 'throughput_comparison', title: 'Throughput Comparison', position: { w: 12, h: 4 } },
